behavior_summary: When an emitter item enters a fast-trigger area, U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea marks the item (info/process-exclude), sets its visible frame to 0, and immediately spawns an asynchronous worker (U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC) to drive ongoing visual/sound effects and nearby-NPC interactions.

The spawned worker (U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC) repeatedly spawns short-lived effect processes (FREE::1C19 variants), randomizes and updates the emitter's animation/frame using urandom + Item::setFrame, suspends between spawns, and launches a short secondary animation worker (U8P_LITZAPEW::01C3) for more complex frame/sound sequences.

U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC also scans nearby map cells: for each overlapping alive NPC it computes a randomized direction (Npc::getDir + urandom) and invokes METHOD::0080 with the emitter and target cell to apply a directional interaction (likely knockback/orientation/movement).

U8P_LITZAPEW::01C3 performs a short timed animation: plays a sound (playSFX), cycles frames (3..5) with interleaved FREE::1C19 spawns, then sets a final frame sequence (2,1,0) with pauses and effect spawns before ending.

When the actor/item leaves the fast area, U8P_LITZAPEW::leaveFastArea clears the per-item state (info/process-exclude semantics) and calls FREE::2A56(item, 0x06) to perform cleanup/teardown for the effect.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea returns immediately after setting item state and spawning U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC; all ongoing behavior runs in spawned processes (asynchronous/process-based flow).
U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC contains a repeating spawn-and-wait loop: spawn FREE::1C19 effects, randomize frame, suspend, and occasionally spawn the secondary worker U8P_LITZAPEW::01C3.
U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC iterates over nearby map offsets/cells and conditionally applies METHOD::0080 to overlapping, living NPCs (Item::overlaps, Item::isNpc, Npc::isDead checks).
Randomization (urandom) is used for timing, frame selection, and direction offsets, producing non-deterministic visuals/interaction directions.
open_questions: Exact semantics of FREE::1C19: spawned repeatedly as a short-lived effect; is it a visual particle, projectile, or damage-dealing object? (inferred, not certain).
Precise effect of METHOD::0080 on NPCs: patterns indicate movement/knockback or orientation change, but exact mechanics (damage vs. displacement vs. facing) are not explicit.

Meaning and lifetime of the per-item 'info' and process-exclude flags set/cleared by the script (which info id is used in some places, e.g., 0x210 observed elsewhere) and whether leaveFastArea always cancels all spawned workers reliably.
Under what conditions the primary worker U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC terminates: is it explicitly killed by the cleanup call (FREE::2A56) on leave, or does it self-terminate on internal criteria?
Precise parameter semantics for FREE::2A56(item, 0x06): inferred to be cleanup, but the meaning of code 0x06 is uncertain.

main_function.name: U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: Initialize an emitter item when an actor enters its fast-trigger region: set per-item state, set initial animation frame, and spawn an asynchronous worker to perform repeated visual/sound effects and interact with nearby NPCs.
entry_conditions: Fast-area entry event for the emitter item (U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea).
The triggering actor/item instance is passed as the function parameter (item at BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry handler that marks the item, sets its initial frame (0), and spawns the primary worker U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC to run ongoing effects asynchronously.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPEW::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler that clears per-item state and invokes external cleanup FREE::2A56(item, 0x06).

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC
function_map.role: Primary spawned worker: drives repeated effect spawns (FREE::1C19), randomizes/sets item frames, scans nearby cells and applies METHOD::0080 to alive NPCs with randomized directions, and occasionally spawns the secondary animation helper U8P_LITZAPEW::01C3.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPEW::01C3
function_map.role: Secondary spawned helper: short animation and sound sequence — plays SFX, cycles frames (3..5) with interleaved FREE::1C19 spawns, then transitions frames back to idle before returning.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the asynchronous worker that performs the ongoing visual/effect logic and NPC interactions.
Spawn a short secondary animation worker to run a frame/sound sequence concurrently with the primary loop.

external_calls.purpose: Set the emitter item's visible animation frame to 0 on initialization.
Spawn the primary worker (U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC) and receive its pid for bookkeeping.
Invoke external cleanup/free routine for the item with a small code (0x06) when the item leaves the fast area.
Repeatedly create FREE::1C19 effect processes (variants controlled by a byte argument) as part of the visual/effect loop.
Update the emitter's visible frame to randomized values for animation variability.
Produce random values used to vary timing, frame offsets, and NPC direction offsets.
Test whether the emitter overlaps specific nearby map cells when searching for affected entities.
Determine if an overlapping item is an NPC before applying NPC-specific logic.
Skip dead NPCs when applying interactions.
Obtain an NPC's current direction as a base for a randomized direction offset.
Apply a directional interaction (likely knockback/orientation/movement) to a nearby NPC using the emitter, target cell, and computed direction.
Play a sound effect at the emitter as part of the short animation sequence.
Spawn additional FREE::1C19 effect processes between animation frame changes to enrich visuals/timing.
Cycle the emitter's frames (3..5 then 2,1,0) during the short animation helper.
Clear or reapply per-item info and process-exclude state as part of cleanup (inferred engine ops).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When an emitter item enters a fast-trigger area, U8P_LITZAPEW::enterFastArea marks the item (info/process-exclude), sets its visible frame to 0, and immediately spawns an asynchronous worker (U8P_LITZAPEW::00BC) to drive ongoing visual/sound effects and nearby-NPC interactions.
